changed_functions.name: WskTdiCreateCO (unpatched @ 0x1C00480F8, patched @ 0x1C0048298)
key_changes: Adds a device-identity check after IoGetRelatedDeviceObject, guarded by a feature-staging flag. Patched: at 0x1C00483E8 calls EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_2881162555_60797108_FeatureDescriptorDetails); at 0x1C00483EF 'jz loc_1C004847F' jumps to the unchanged AfdIssueDeviceControl path when the feature is off. When on, it calls IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef([rdi+0x10]) and compares against the cached expected device at [rsi+0x28]; on mismatch sets ebx=0xC0000184 (STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_STATE) and tears down.

The unpatched version stores IoGetRelatedDeviceObject(FileObject) at [rsi+0x10] and proceeds to AfdIssueDeviceControl with no device comparison. This exact path is preserved in the patched build's feature-off branch.
AfdIssueDeviceControl call target is a relocated equivalent (function moved), not a behavior change.
IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef is not a new import: it already exists at five call sites in the unpatched binary.
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-staged defense-in-depth device-identity check, not a delivered fix. The pre-patch behavior is retained intact behind the disabled-feature branch (jz 0x1C004847F). The check verifies that the opened transport device's attachment base matches a device cached earlier at offset 0x28. Producing a mismatch requires attaching a filter driver to, or redirecting the namespace name of, the transport device stack, which are administrative operations; an unprivileged Winsock caller opens a fixed transport device for the requested protocol and cannot steer AFD to an unexpected device. No memory-corruption or type-confusion consequence is demonstrable from either build.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED WskTdiCreateCO @ 0x1C00480F8 (no identity check):
0x1C0048229: call IoGetRelatedDeviceObject
0x1C0048248: mov [rsi+10h], rax        ; store device object
0x1C0048255: call AfdIssueDeviceControl ; proceed, no comparison
0x1C0048260: xor eax, eax               ; return SUCCESS

PATCHED WskTdiCreateCO @ 0x1C0048298 (feature-gated check):
0x1C00483D1: call IoGetRelatedDeviceObject
0x1C00483DD: lea rcx, g_Feature_2881162555_60797108_FeatureDescriptorDetails
0x1C00483E4: mov [rdi+10h], rax
0x1C00483E8: call EvaluateCurrentState
0x1C00483EF: jz  loc_1C004847F          ; FEATURE OFF -> unchanged old path
0x1C00483F9: call IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef
0x1C0048408: cmp [rsi+28h], rax         ; compare cached expected device
0x1C004840C: jz  loc_1C0048473          ; match -> proceed
0x1C004841A: mov ebx, 0C0000184h        ; STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_STATE (mismatch)
0x1C004849B: call AfdIssueDeviceControl ; loc_1C004847F: issue path

changed_functions.name: WskTdiCreateAO (unpatched @ 0x1C0047CD4, patched @ 0x1C0047E24)
key_changes: Same feature-gated IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef identity check as WskTdiCreateCO, guarded by EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_2881162555_60797108...) with the pre-patch path retained in the feature-off branch.
Cache field offsets shifted by 8 bytes to accommodate the new cached-device field at offset 0x28.
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-staged variant of the same device-identity check. Not a delivered fix; pre-patch behavior retained behind the feature flag.

changed_functions.name: AfdTdiCreateAO (@ 0x1C003D750)
key_changes: Main transport-create handler. Adds the same feature-gated identity check: 0x1C003E0A3 calls EvaluateCurrentState; 0x1C003E0AA 'jz 0x1C003E0FA' skips the check (old behavior, r15=rdi) when the feature is off; when on, IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef + cmp [r13+0x28] + ebx=0xC0000184 on mismatch.
Cache structure field offsets shifted, reflecting the 8-byte-larger cache entry.
Error path calls the relocated AfdFreeTransportInfo for cache-entry cleanup.
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-staged device-identity check in the main transport-create handler. Pre-patch behavior retained in the feature-off branch (jz 0x1C003E0FA). The cached expected device at offset 0x28 is only populated when the feature is on (see AfdGetTransportInfo).

changed_functions.name: AfdGetTransportInfo (@ 0x1C003B044)
key_changes: Transport-cache entry enlarged by 8 bytes; new device-pointer field at offset 0x28 (P[5]).

The new field is populated only when the feature is on: 0x1C003B4B7 calls EvaluateCurrentState; 0x1C003B4BE 'jz 0x1C003B4E5' skips storing IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef(IoGetRelatedDeviceObject(fileobj)) into [r14] when the feature is off.
Data copy offsets shifted due to the new field; newly allocated entries initialize the new field to 0.
changed_functions.security_notes: Cache-management function that stores the expected base device pointer later compared in the transport-create paths. Storage is feature-gated; when the feature is off the field is never written and no comparison occurs anywhere.

changed_functions.name: AfdQueryProviderInfo (@ 0x1C003B220)
key_changes: Signature gains an output parameter for the base device object.
When the feature is on, retrieves IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef(IoGetRelatedDeviceObject(fileobj)) and returns it via the new output parameter.
Calls the relocated AfdIssueDeviceControl; error path restructured for the new output.
changed_functions.security_notes: Provider/device lookup helper called by AfdGetTransportInfo. Returns the base device object used to seed the feature-gated cache field. Not security-relevant on its own.

changed_functions.name: AfdFreeTransportInfo (@ 0x1C003B300)
key_changes: Adds a feature-gated ObfDereferenceObject of the new cached device pointer (arg1[5] / offset 0x28) when freeing the cache entry, balancing the reference taken by the cache path.
changed_functions.security_notes: Reference cleanup for the feature-gated cached device pointer. Only relevant when the feature is on; balances the added reference. Not an independent security fix.

changed_functions.name: AfdSocketTransferEnd (@ 0x1C0061C30)
key_changes: Bluetooth RFCOMM IRP forwarding handler; reference-management helper functions relocated/renamed, refcount logic reorganized (InterlockedDecrement with conditional cleanup).
IRP minor code 0x520f.

changed_functions.name: AfdSocketTransferBegin (@ 0x1C0061E50)
key_changes: Mirror of AfdSocketTransferEnd with IRP minor code 0x510f. Same relocation and refcount reorganization.

changed_functions.name: AfdWaitForListen (@ 0x1C005DE80)
key_changes: Receive handler with reordered conditional branches.
Cancel routine relocated.
Feature-staging check added in the not-connected path.

changed_functions.name: AfdSanAcceptCore (@ 0x1C007E0B8)
key_changes: SAN (Winsock Direct) receive-completion size handling. Unpatched requires the 12-byte minimum only when feature flag g_Feature_30880057_59779870 is on: at 0x1C007E379 EvaluateCurrentState, then 0x1C007E382 'cmp [r13+0x10], 0Ch; jb skip'. Patched makes the check unconditional: 0x1C007E360 'cmp ebx, 0Ch; jb loc_1C007E533'.
MDL mapping via MmMapLockedPages and buffer copy via memmove.
changed_functions.security_notes: Finalizes a feature-staged minimum-size gate on the SAN accept path (makes the 12-byte minimum unconditional). This is the AFD SAN feature-staging pattern, not the transport-create change, and no impact for the sub-12-byte case is demonstrable. Not security-relevant.

changed_functions.name: AfdTLSendMessage (@ 0x1C0039544)
key_changes: Feature-staging conditional removed: an assignment made unconditional.
Helper function references relocated/renamed.

changed_functions.name: AfdTLSendMsgComplete (@ 0x1C0062070)
key_changes: Feature-staging conditional removed from an IRP-completion test.

changed_functions.name: AfdTLDgramSendToComplete (@ 0x1C0077030)
key_changes: Same feature-staging conditional removal as AfdTLSendMsgComplete.
Variable renames.

changed_functions.name: AfdSanConnectHandler (@ 0x1C007F630)
key_changes: Large function with mostly variable renames and address shifts.

changed_functions.name: AfdTLVcSendDgram (@ 0x1C0062790)
key_changes: Feature-staging conditional removed: assignment made unconditional.
Cancel and completion routine references relocated.

changed_functions.name: AfdTLVcSendDgramComplete (@ 0x1C00629A0)
key_changes: Same feature-staging conditional removal as AfdTLSendMsgComplete.

changed_functions.name: AfdGetInformation (@ 0x1C00389B0)
key_changes: Variable renames and address shifts in large IOCTL dispatch function.

changed_functions.name: AfdSendDatagram (@ 0x1C00765A0)
key_changes: IRP dispatch handler with variable renames and function reference updates.
Feature-staging conditional removed for an assignment.

changed_functions.name: AfdUnload (@ 0x1C0040330)
key_changes: Driver unload: adds a feature-staging teardown call and relocated references.

changed_functions.name: AfdBind (@ 0x1C0037A80)
key_changes: Address shifts and variable renames in large IOCTL dispatch function.

changed_functions.name: AfdSend (@ 0x1C0050950)
key_changes: Receive IRP handler with restructured error handling and status-code ordering changes (STATUS_LOCAL_DISCONNECT / STATUS_PIPE_DISCONNECTED).

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0052029
key_changes: Large function with structural reorganization and variable renames.

security_findings.function: WskTdiCreateCO / WskTdiCreateAO / AfdTdiCreateAO / AfdGetTransportInfo / AfdQueryProviderInfo / AfdFreeTransportInfo
security_findings.entry_point: AFD device IOCTL (NtDeviceIoControlFile on \Device\Afd) via Winsock connect/bind operations
call_chain: WskTdiCreateCO or WskTdiCreateAO (device open via IoCreateFile)
IoGetRelatedDeviceObject (device stored; feature-gated identity check added in patched build)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Feature-staged defense-in-depth device-identity check (no delivered fix)
security_findings.description: The patch stages a new device-identity check in the AFD transport-create and transport-cache paths. In the unpatched build, WskTdiCreateCO (@ 0x1C00480F8) opens the transport device via IoCreateFile, references the FILE_OBJECT, obtains the device via IoGetRelatedDeviceObject, stores it, and issues the control request with no device comparison. In the patched build, WskTdiCreateCO (relocated to @ 0x1C0048298), WskTdiCreateAO, and AfdTdiCreateAO add, after IoGetRelatedDeviceObject, a check guarded by EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_2881162555_60797108_FeatureDescriptorDetails). When the feature is off, the code branches directly to the unchanged pre-patch request path (jz 0x1C004847F in WskTdiCreateCO). When on, it calls IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef on the opened device and compares the attachment-base against a device pointer cached earlier at offset 0x28 (populated by AfdGetTransportInfo/AfdQueryProviderInfo only when the feature is on, and released by AfdFreeTransportInfo); on mismatch it returns STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_STATE (0xC0000184). Because the pre-patch behavior is fully retained behind the disabled-feature branch, this is a staged rollout, not a delivered remediation. IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef is not a new import; it already exists at five call sites in the unpatched binary.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: For the added check to ever fail, the transport device AFD opens must resolve to, or be intercepted by, a device other than the expected transport. Producing that state requires attaching a filter driver to the transport device stack or redirecting a \Device\ transport name in the Object Manager namespace, both administrative operations. An unprivileged process performing a normal Winsock connect/bind opens a fixed transport device determined by the protocol catalog and cannot steer AFD to an unexpected device. No unprivileged trigger produces a mismatch or any fault.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrable from the binaries. The opened device is fixed by the protocol catalog for an unprivileged caller; no attacker-controlled input steers device selection, and neither build demonstrates any memory-corruption or type-confusion consequence. The only way to make the opened device differ from the expected one is itself an administrative capability, so the check is defense-in-depth robustness rather than a fix for an unprivileged-reachable flaw.
security_findings.evidence:
UNPATCHED WskTdiCreateCO @ 0x1C00480F8:
0x1C0048229: call IoGetRelatedDeviceObject
0x1C0048248: mov [rsi+10h], rax        ; store device object (no comparison)
0x1C0048255: call AfdIssueDeviceControl
0x1C0048260: xor eax, eax               ; return SUCCESS

PATCHED WskTdiCreateCO @ 0x1C0048298:
0x1C00483D1: call IoGetRelatedDeviceObject
0x1C00483DD: lea rcx, g_Feature_2881162555_60797108_FeatureDescriptorDetails
0x1C00483E4: mov [rdi+10h], rax
0x1C00483E8: call EvaluateCurrentState
0x1C00483EF: jz  loc_1C004847F          ; FEATURE OFF -> unchanged old path
0x1C00483F9: call IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef
0x1C0048408: cmp [rsi+28h], rax
0x1C004840C: jz  loc_1C0048473          ; match -> proceed
0x1C004841A: mov ebx, 0C0000184h        ; STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_STATE (mismatch)
0x1C004849B: call AfdIssueDeviceControl ; loc_1C004847F: issue path

Same construct in AfdTdiCreateAO (EvaluateCurrentState @ 0x1C003E0A3, jz 0x1C003E0FA, IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef @ 0x1C003E0C1, cmp [r13+28h]) and AfdGetTransportInfo (EvaluateCurrentState @ 0x1C003B4B7, jz 0x1C003B4E5, IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef @ 0x1C003B4D6, store to [r14]).
security_findings.assembly: See evidence field. The load-bearing detail is the 'jz' after EvaluateCurrentState in each function, which routes the feature-off case to the unchanged pre-patch path, and the fact that the cached expected-device field (offset 0x28) is only written when the feature is on.

breakpoints.why: Call to EvaluateCurrentState. eax==0 (jz 0x1C004847F) routes to the unchanged AfdIssueDeviceControl path; eax!=0 runs IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef and the cmp [rsi+28h] identity comparison at 0x1C0048408.
IoGetRelatedDeviceObject call; the returned device is stored at [rsi+10h] (0x1C0048248) and the request proceeds at 0x1C0048255 with no comparison.
EvaluateCurrentState in the main transport-create handler; jz 0x1C003E0FA skips the IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef / cmp [r13+28h] check when the feature is off.

watch: eax after EvaluateCurrentState at 0x1C00483E8 (patched) = feature state; zero means the identity check is skipped and behavior equals unpatched
[rsi+0x28] (patched) = cached expected base device; only populated when the feature is on
rax after IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef = attachment base compared against the cached device
key_offsets: 0x1C00483EF (patched): jz 0x1C004847F — feature-off routes to the unchanged old path
0x1C0048408 (patched): cmp [rsi+28h], rax — the feature-gated device identity comparison
0x1C0048229 (unpatched): call IoGetRelatedDeviceObject — device obtained, no comparison follows
0x1C003B4BE (patched AfdGetTransportInfo): jz 0x1C003B4E5 — cached device stored only when the feature is on
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Open an AFD socket (WSASocket or NtCreateFile on \Device\Afd) and issue connect/bind to drive AfdTdiCreateAO -> WskTdiCreateCO/WskTdiCreateAO. For an unprivileged caller the opened device is the fixed transport device for the requested protocol; when the feature is on the comparison matches and behavior equals the unpatched build. There is no unprivileged way to make the opened device differ from the expected one.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: For a normal unprivileged connect/bind, execution with the feature on reaches the match path (cmp [rsi+28h] equal) and proceeds identically to the unpatched build; with the feature off it takes the jz to the unchanged path. No fault or corruption is produced.
debugging_aids.notes: This is a Windows feature-staging (Velocity) rollout: g_Feature_2881162555_60797108 gates the new device-identity check across the transport-create and transport-cache functions, and the pre-patch code path is retained in every feature-off branch. Prior framing as CWE-843 type confusion / CWE-1235 with arbitrary read/write and local privilege escalation is not supported by either build: it required administrative preconditions (filter-driver attach or namespace redirection of a transport device) and a memory-corruption consequence that neither build demonstrates.

summary: The patch does not deliver a reachable security fix. It stages a new device-identity check in the AFD transport-create path (WskTdiCreateCO, WskTdiCreateAO, AfdTdiCreateAO) and the supporting transport-cache helpers (AfdGetTransportInfo, AfdQueryProviderInfo, AfdFreeTransportInfo). Every new check is guarded by a Windows feature-staging flag, EvaluateCurrentState(g_Feature_2881162555_60797108...), with the exact pre-patch code path retained in the feature-disabled branch (WskTdiCreateCO jz 0x1C004847F, AfdTdiCreateAO jz 0x1C003E0FA, AfdGetTransportInfo jz 0x1C003B4E5). The check verifies the opened transport device's attachment base against a device cached at offset 0x28. Producing a mismatch requires attaching a filter driver to, or redirecting the namespace name of, the transport device stack, which are administrative operations not available to an unprivileged Winsock caller. IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef is not a new import (already present at five sites unpatched). No memory-corruption, type-confusion, or privilege-escalation impact is demonstrable from the binaries. The remaining changed functions are feature-staging finalizations, IRP-handler reorganizations, and relocations/renames. The AfdSanAcceptCore change makes a 12-byte minimum size gate unconditional on the SAN accept path (feature-staging finalization), with no demonstrable impact.
